channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Manager of botnet used in ransomware attacks gets 2 years in prison
item.description: A Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison after admitting that the phishing botnet he managed was used to launch BitPaymer ransomware attacks against 72 U.S. companies. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over Supply Chain and Cyber Risk Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it was banning the import of new, foreign-made consumer routers, citing "unacceptable" risks to cyber and national security. The ac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran Hacktivists Make Noise but Have Little Impact on War
item.description: Iran-aligned groups are trying to make their mark in the Gulf, but the results have fallen short of remarkable....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: PTC warns of imminent threat from critical Windchill, FlexPLM RCE bug
item.description: PTC Inc. is warning of a critical vulnerability in Windchill and FlexPLM, widely used product lifecycle management (PLM) solutions, that could allow remote code execution. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Popular LiteLLM PyPI package backdoored to steal credentials, auth tokens
item.description: The TeamPCP hacking group continues its supply-chain rampage, now compromising the massively popular "LiteLLM" Python package on PyPI and claiming to have stolen data from hundreds of thousands of dev...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Checkmarx KICS Code Scanner Targeted in Widening Supply Chain Hit
item.description: TeamPCP is the likely cyber threat actor behind attacks on Trivy, Checkmarx's KICS and VS Code plug-ins, and the LiteLLM AI library — and all signs point to more attacks to come....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: How AI Coding Tools Crushed the Endpoint Security Fortress
item.description: Security vendors have spent years building up defenses around the endpoint, but one researcher says AI coding tools have brought the walls down....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FCC bans new routers made outside the USA over security risks
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has updated its Covered List to include all consumer routers made in foreign countries, banning the sale of new models in the U.S. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential harvester,...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Firefox now has a free built-in VPN with 50GB monthly data limit
item.description: Mozilla released Firefox 149 with added privacy protection through a built-in VPN tool offering up to 50GB of monthly traffic. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tax Search Ads Deliver ScreenConnect Malware Using Huawei Driver to Disable EDR
item.description: A large-scale malvertising campaign active since January 2026 has been observed targeting U.S.-based individuals searching for tax-related documents to serve rogue installers for ConnectWise ScreenCon...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 5 Learnings from the First-Ever Gartner Market Guide for Guardian Agents
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Gartner published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, marking an important milestone for this emerging category. For those unfamiliar with the various Gartner report ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Resumes to Steal Enterprise Credentials and Deploy Crypto Miner
item.description: An ongoing phishing campaign is targeting French-speaking corporate environments with fake resumes that lead to the deployment of cryptocurrency miners and information stealers. "The campaign uses hig...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes bug causing Classic Outlook sync issues with Gmail
item.description: ​Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing Gmail and Yahoo email synchronization and connection problems for classic Outlook users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GitHub 'OpenClaw Deployer' Repo Delivers Trojan Instead
item.description: An AI-assisted campaign is spreading more than 300 poisoned packages for diverse assets ranging from developer tools to game cheats....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Zero Trust: Bridging the Gap Between Authentication and Trust
item.description: Passing MFA doesn't mean a session is safe, attackers can hijack tokens and bypass identity checks. Specops Software explains why Zero Trust must verify both user identity and device health. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: HackerOne discloses employee data breach after Navia hack
item.description: Bug bounty platform HackerOne is notifying hundreds of employees that their data was stolen after attackers hacked Navia, one of its U.S. benefits administrators. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Infinite Campus warns of breach after ShinyHunters claims data theft
item.description: Infinite Campus, a widely used K-12 student information system, is warning customers of a data breach following an extortion attempt by a threat actor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How a Large Bank Uses AI Digital Twins for Threat Hunting
item.description: JPMorgan Chase uses digital fingerprints and digital twins to spot online attackers and malicious behaviors while also reducing pesky false alerts....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Yanluowang ransomware access broker gets 81 months in prison
item.description: A Russian national was sentenced to nearly 7 years in prison after pleading guilty to acting as an initial access broker (IAB) for Yanluowang ransomware attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Proposes Better Identity, Guardrails for AI Agents
item.description: Companies need better controls to manage key threats rising from the growth of agentic AI. These new features provide a starting point....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Dutch Ministry of Finance discloses breach affecting employees
item.description: The Dutch Ministry of Finance confirmed on Monday that some of its systems were breached in a cyberattack detected last week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same basic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description: Two more GitHub Actions workflows have become the latest to be compromised by credential-stealing malware by a threat actor known as TeamPCP, the cloud-native cybercriminal operation also behind the T...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: U.S. Sentences Russian Hacker to 6.75 Years for Role in $9M Ransomware Damage
item.description: A 26-year-old Russian citizen has been sentenced in the U.S. to 6.75 years (81 months) in prison for his role in assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware crew, in conduct...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
item.description: Citrix has released security updates to address two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway, including a critical flaw that could be exploited to leak sensitive data from the applicatio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Library to store your personal files
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a new feature called 'Library' for ChatGPT, which allows you to store your personal files or images on OpenAI's cloud storage, so you can reference those items in a future chat. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mazda discloses security breach exposing employee and partner data
item.description: Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda) announced that information belonging to its employees and business partners had been exposed in a security incident detected last December. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI in the SOC: What Could Go Wrong?
item.description: Two cybersecurity leaders tested out AI in their respective SOCs for six months — and here's what they learned....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
